title: SVGR
subtitle: Convert raster images to scalable vector graphics
description: Ideal for converting AI-generated logos. Since all logo generators create raster files, scaling and editing become difficult. SVGR converts them to vector format, giving designers a starting point to fine-tune — even if the result isn't pixel-perfect.

useCases.title: Why Use SVGR?
useCases.subtitle: SVGR converts raster images (PNG, JPG) into scalable vector graphics (SVG) — saving you time and money.

aiLogos.title: Convert AI-Generated Logos to Vector
aiLogos.description: Every AI logo generator creates raster images (PNG/JPG). These images lose quality when scaled, look blurry on large displays, and can't be cleanly edited in vector design tools. To use an AI-generated logo professionally — on business cards, banners, merchandise, or websites — you need a vector version.
aiLogos.problemDescription: AI logo generators only output raster files. Raster images are not scalable, not suitable for high-resolution printing, and difficult to edit. Hiring a graphic designer to manually recreate the logo as a vector costs money and takes time.
aiLogos.solutionDescription: SVGR converts your AI-generated logo into an SVG file instantly. Even for complex designs, SVGR creates a solid first draft that significantly reduces manual work — giving designers a clean starting point to fine-tune rather than starting from scratch.

legacyAssets.title: Modernize Legacy Image Assets
legacyAssets.description: Many organizations have older image assets — logos, icons, diagrams, and illustrations — stored only as raster files. These assets may have been created years ago when vector tools were less accessible, or the original source files have been lost over time.
legacyAssets.problemDescription: Legacy raster images degrade when resized, look pixelated on modern high-DPI screens, and can't be easily edited or recolored. Recreating them from scratch is time-consuming and expensive.
legacyAssets.solutionDescription: SVGR lets you batch-convert old raster assets into clean SVG files. The result is a scalable, editable vector that works perfectly on any screen size — from mobile devices to 4K displays and print.

tutorials.title: Tutorials
tutorials.subtitle: Step-by-step guides for converting different types of images to SVG.

ai-logos-to-svg.title: Convert AI Logos to SVG
ai-logos-to-svg.description: Vectorize AI-generated logos for scalable use
ai-logos-to-svg.intro: All AI logo generators — Looka, Brandmark, Hatchful, Logo.com, and others — output raster images (PNG or JPG). Raster logos lose quality when scaled up for banners, merchandise, or print. They appear blurry on high-DPI screens and cannot be edited cleanly in vector design tools like Illustrator or Figma. To use an AI-generated logo professionally, you need a scalable vector (SVG) version.

steps.title: How to Convert
steps.step1: Upload your AI-generated logo image (PNG, JPG, or WEBP).
steps.step2: Set Image Type to Design. This tells SVGR to preserve hard edges and flat color regions typical of logo artwork.
steps.step3: Set Quality Level to 5. This captures fine detail without over-complicating the SVG paths. You can adjust up or down depending on the complexity of your logo.
steps.step4: Click Convert and preview the result.

tips.title: Tips
tips.tip1: Use a logo image with a transparent or solid background for the cleanest result.
tips.tip2: If the result looks too simplified, increase the quality level. If it looks too complex, decrease it.
tips.tip3: The SVG output is a great starting point for designers to fine-tune in Illustrator or Figma — even if it is not pixel-perfect.

ai-posters-to-svg.title: Convert AI Posters to SVG
ai-posters-to-svg.description: Vectorize AI-generated posters for large-format print
ai-posters-to-svg.intro: AI image generators like Midjourney, DALL-E, and Stable Diffusion create stunning poster artwork — but they output raster images. Raster images have a fixed pixel resolution and become blurry when printed at large sizes. For posters, banners, and signage, you need a vector format that scales to any size without quality loss.

steps.title: How to Convert
steps.step1: Upload your AI-generated poster image (PNG, JPG, or WEBP).
steps.step2: Set Image Type to Design. This mode preserves the graphic style and color regions of poster artwork.
steps.step3: Set Quality Level to 4. Poster artwork is typically less detailed than logos, so a lower quality level keeps file size manageable. Adjust upward if you need more detail.
steps.step4: Click Convert and preview the result.

warning.title: File Size Warning
warning.text: AI-generated poster artwork can produce large SVG files because of the complexity and number of colors. A typical poster conversion may result in an SVG file of several megabytes. This is normal for detailed artwork. If the file is too large, try reducing the quality level.

tips.title: Tips
tips.tip1: Start with quality level 4 and only increase if the preview lacks important detail.
tips.tip2: Simpler poster designs (fewer colors, bold shapes) produce smaller and cleaner SVGs.
tips.tip3: The converted SVG can be printed at any size — from A4 to billboard — without quality loss.

qr-code-to-svg.title: Convert QR Code to SVG
qr-code-to-svg.description: Vectorize QR codes for crisp large-format printing
qr-code-to-svg.intro: Many QR code generators output raster images (PNG or JPG). A raster QR code works fine on screens, but becomes pixelated when printed large — on banners, posters, vehicle wraps, or signage. This is especially problematic for QR codes with embedded custom graphics or logos, where the detail is lost at low resolution. Converting to SVG ensures your QR code stays crisp and scannable at any print size.

steps.title: How to Convert
steps.step1: Upload your QR code image (PNG, JPG, or WEBP).
steps.step2: Set Image Type to Design. QR codes are graphic patterns with hard edges, so the Design mode produces the cleanest result.
steps.step3: Set Quality Level to 5. This captures the sharp edges and fine detail of QR patterns. Adjust if needed — lower for simple QR codes, higher for QR codes with embedded logos.
steps.step4: Click Convert and preview the result.

tips.title: Tips
tips.tip1: Always test your converted QR code by scanning it before using it in production. Make sure it still scans correctly after conversion.
tips.tip2: QR codes with embedded logos or custom colors may need a higher quality level (6-7) to preserve the embedded graphic.
tips.tip3: The SVG QR code can be resized to any dimension — from business cards to billboards — and will remain scannable.

photo-to-svg.title: Convert Photo to SVG
photo-to-svg.description: Transform photos into abstract vector art
photo-to-svg.intro: Converting a photograph to SVG produces a stylized, abstract art effect. Unlike logos or QR codes, photos contain continuous tones and gradients that cannot be represented exactly in vector format. The result is an artistic interpretation — shapes and color regions that capture the essence of the photo in a painterly or posterized style. This is ideal for creative projects, wall art, or stylized illustrations based on real photos.

steps.title: How to Convert
steps.step1: Upload your photo (PNG, JPG, or WEBP).
steps.step2: Set Image Type to Photo. This mode is optimized for continuous-tone images and produces smoother color transitions.
steps.step3: Disable Recognize Text. Photos typically don't contain text that needs to be preserved as editable paths.
steps.step4: Click Convert and preview the result. The output will have an abstract, artistic quality.

warning.title: File Size Warning
warning.text: Photo-to-SVG conversion can produce large files because photographs contain many color regions and gradients. A high-resolution photo may result in an SVG of several megabytes. For smaller files, use a lower-resolution source image.

tips.title: Tips
tips.tip1: Photos with strong contrast, bold colors, and simple compositions produce the best results.
tips.tip2: The result is not a photorealistic reproduction — it is an artistic vector interpretation. Embrace the abstract quality.
tips.tip3: Try different source photos to see which styles work best. Portraits, landscapes, and still life subjects each produce distinct results.

cta.subtitle: Upload your image and convert it to SVG in seconds.
